message:
fix(fwc): offline preflight returns success exit code with offline-artifact provenance

The offline-artifact analysis path in preflight_dispatch_without_host was
returning CliExitCode::Transport even when it successfully produced a
structured offline analysis payload with CommandAvailability::OfflineArtifact.
This made the hybrid degraded path look like a connectivity failure to agents
and scripts despite delivering useful output.

Changes:
- Return CliExitCode::Success instead of CliExitCode::Transport from
  preflight_dispatch_without_host when the offline analysis completes
- Update the regression test to assert Success exit code and verify the
  availability field contains "offline-artifact"
- Align cual_bench stubs to use MetadataField::Known(bool) for
  supports_simulate and has_events fields, matching the current type
  signatures after the MetadataField refactor

Also adds beads tracker issue flywheel_connectors-5w1wb tracking this fix.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-5szl","title":"TEST: Agent Integration Unit Tests (Introspection, Tool Descriptors, Discovery)","description":"# TEST: Agent Integration Unit Tests

## Goal
Verify that FCP2 operations are discoverable and safe for AI agents.

## Test Categories

### 1. Introspection Schema Tests
- Introspection object schema validation (canonical CBOR)
- All required fields present (operations, schemas, risk levels)
- Golden vectors for introspection responses

### 2. Operation Discovery Tests
- Enumerate all connector operations
- Each operation has input/output schemas
- Risk tier correctly reported
- Rate limits correctly reported

### 3. MCP Tool Descriptor Tests
- Mapping to MCP-compatible tool descriptors
- Tool name, description, examples present
- Input/output schema translation
- Risk tier to MCP safety level mapping

### 4. E2E Discovery Tests
- Agent can enumerate tools from connector
- Agent can invoke safe operation end-to-end
- Agent can read recovery hints on failure

## Logging Requirements
- Structured JSON with operation_id, schema versions
- Discovery timing metrics
- Error details with recovery hints

## Acceptance Criteria
- Introspection is complete for agent operation
- No hardcoded connector knowledge required
- Tool descriptors match MCP conventions","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-15T08:50:32.192813258Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-28T20:59:34.312640984Z","closed_at":"2026-01-28T20:59:34.312513066Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["agent","fcp2","mvp","testing"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-5szl","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-1n78.30","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-02T18:31:41Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-5szl","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-1n78.35","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-02T18:31:41Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-5un1","title":"TEST: fcp-telemetry Unit Test Suite","description":"# TEST: fcp-telemetry Unit Test Suite


## Goal
Validate `fcp-telemetry` emits spec-required metrics/logging fields, preserves correlation, and never leaks secrets.

## Scope
Comprehensive unit tests for metrics, logging, and tracing library.

## Test Categories

### Structured Logging
- [ ] Log level filtering
- [ ] JSON output format
- [ ] Field injection (correlation IDs, zone, connector)
- [ ] Sensitive data redaction
- [ ] Log rotation handling

### Metrics Collection
- [ ] Counter increments
- [ ] Gauge values
- [ ] Histogram distributions
- [ ] Timing measurements
- [ ] Label/tag handling

### Distributed Tracing
- [ ] Span creation and nesting
- [ ] Context propagation
- [ ] Trace ID generation
- [ ] Span attributes
- [ ] Sampling decisions

### Export
- [ ] Prometheus format export
- [ ] OTLP format export
- [ ] Custom sink support
- [ ] Batch flushing

### Performance
- [ ] Low overhead in hot path
- [ ] Async export
- [ ] Buffer management

## Coverage Target: >90%

## Acceptance Criteria
- Runs deterministically in CI (no flake due to timing/network).
- Uses mocks only (no real external network).
- Emits structured JSON logs per `flywheel_connectors-1n78.35` with stable `test_name` identifiers.

 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-5vy","title":"fcp.webhook-receiver: Inbound Event Gateway","description":"# Inbound Event Gateway Meta-Connector

**Force Multiplier:** Universal webhook receiver for external service integrations.

## Overview

Many services push events via webhooks (GitHub, Stripe, Discord, Slack, etc.). Instead of building separate webhook handlers for each service, this meta-connector provides a unified HTTP endpoint infrastructure with per-service configuration.

## Core Components

### 1. HTTP Endpoint Management
- Dynamic endpoint creation/deletion
- Unique URL per webhook source
- Support for path-based routing (`/webhooks/{service}/{id}`)
- Health check endpoints
- CORS and preflight handling
- Rate limiting per endpoint

### 2. Signature Verification (Per-Service)
- Pluggable verification strategies:
  - HMAC-SHA256 (GitHub, Stripe)
  - HMAC-SHA1 (legacy services)
  - Ed25519 (Discord)
  - API key header
  - Basic auth
  - Custom verification functions
- Timing-safe comparison
- Signature caching for replay prevention
- Failed verification alerting

### 3. Payload Transformation
- JSON schema validation
- Payload normalization to common event format
- Field mapping/extraction
- Nested payload unwrapping
- Binary payload handling (multipart)
- Compression support (gzip, deflate)

### 4. Event Routing to Zones
- Route events to appropriate FCP zones
- Support multiple destinations per event type
- Filter events based on payload content
- Priority-based routing
- Fan-out to multiple handlers

### 5. Retry/Dead-Letter Handling
- Configurable retry strategies:
  - Exponential backoff
  - Fixed interval
  - Fibonacci backoff
- Dead letter queue for failed events
- Manual retry interface
- Event replay capability
- TTL-based expiration

## Configuration Example

```toml
[[webhooks]]
name = "github-repo-events"
path = "/webhooks/github/main-repo"
signature_type = "hmac-sha256"
signature_header = "X-Hub-Signature-256"
secret = "${GITHUB_WEBHOOK_SECRET}"
route_to = "zone.github-automation"
event_type_header = "X-GitHub-Event"

[[webhooks]]
name = "stripe-payments"
path = "/webhooks/stripe/payments"
signature_type = "stripe"
secret = "${STRIPE_WEBHOOK_SECRET}"
route_to = "zone.billing"
retry_max = 5
retry_backoff = "exponential"

[[webhooks]]
name = "discord-interactions"
path = "/webhooks/discord/bot"
signature_type = "ed25519"
public_key = "${DISCORD_PUBLIC_KEY}"
route_to = "zone.discord-bot"
```

## Event Format (Normalized)

```json
{
  "id": "evt_abc123",
  "source": "github",
  "type": "push",
  "timestamp": "2024-01-15T10:30:00Z",
  "webhook_id": "github-repo-events",
  "payload": { ... },
  "metadata": {
    "delivery_id": "xyz789",
    "signature_valid": true,
    "received_at": "2024-01-15T10:30:00.123Z"
  }
}
```

## Success Criteria

- [ ] Support 10+ common webhook signature types
- [ ] Sub-50ms processing latency
- [ ] 99.9% uptime for webhook endpoints
- [ ] Complete audit trail for all received webhooks
- [ ] Zero data loss with dead-letter queue
- [ ] Hot-reload of webhook configurations

## Dependencies

- HTTP server framework (axum/actix-web)
- Persistent queue for dead-letter handling
- Zone routing system

## Security Considerations

- All secrets stored encrypted
- IP allowlisting per webhook (optional)
- Request size limits
- Timeout handling
- DDoS protection integration","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-01-11T15:23:06.647234418Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-11T15:53:35.455928212Z","closed_at":"2026-01-11T15:53:35.455928212Z","close_reason":"Duplicate of flywheel_connectors-3md (fcp.webhook-receiver: Inbound Event Gateway Connector)","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
+{"id":"flywheel_connectors-5w1wb","title":"[fwc] offline preflight should succeed with offline-artifact exit semantics","description":"fwc preflight has an explicit offline-artifact analysis path in crates/fwc/src/main.rs, but preflight_dispatch_without_host currently returns CliExitCode::Transport even when it successfully emits a structured offline analysis payload with CommandAvailability::OfflineArtifact. This makes the hybrid degraded path look like a connectivity failure to agents and scripts despite delivering useful output. Acceptance: offline fwc preflight returns success semantics with preserved offline-artifact provenance, and a regression test locks the behavior.","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-03-27T17:37:59.574831Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-03-27T17:38:17.702494Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["fwc","preflight","truthfulness"]}

 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-5y7","title":"fcp-config: Configuration Validation and Schema Framework","description":"# fcp-config: Configuration Validation and Schema Framework

## Overview
Type-safe configuration framework with JSON Schema validation, secret handling, and environment variable support for FCP connectors.

## Schema Validation
- JSON Schema Draft 7 validation
- Required field enforcement
- Type validation (string, number, bool, array, object)
- Format validation (uri, email, uuid, datetime)
- Custom validators
- Nested schema support

## Configuration Loading
- TOML file parsing
- JSON file parsing
- Environment variable expansion (${VAR})
- File path resolution
- Default value injection
- Configuration merging

## Secret Handling
- Secret placeholder syntax: ${secret:name}
- Environment variable secrets
- File-based secrets (Docker secrets)
- Vault integration (future)
- No secrets in logs/errors

## Validation Errors
- Human-readable error messages
- JSON path to invalid field
- Expected vs actual type
- Suggested fixes
- Multiple error aggregation

## Configuration Schema Definition
- Derive macros for Rust structs
- Automatic schema generation
- Documentation extraction

## Features
- Hot reload support
- Change detection
- Validation caching
- Cross-field validation

## Dependencies
- serde, serde_json, toml
- jsonschema, regex
- fcp-core","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-01-11T15:49:58.772040571Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-15T08:22:33.857911504Z","closed_at":"2026-01-15T08:22:33.857911504Z","close_reason":"Superseded by FCP2 Connector Manifest (1n78.19). Configuration validation is now part of manifest parsing/verification.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-5yw","title":"fcp.figma: Component extraction and inspection","description":"# fcp.figma: Component extraction and inspection (FCP2)

## Goal
Expose Figma component metadata so agents can:
- identify reusable components and variants
- inspect component properties (auto-layout, constraints)
- support design-to-code workflows

## Scope
- List components and component sets for a file or library.
- Fetch component details:
  - name
  - key/id
  - description
  - variant properties (for sets)
- Find component instances within a file (where supported).

## Performance constraints
- Figma files can be very large; component discovery must be:
  - paginated
  - bounded memory
  - avoid downloading full node trees unless explicitly requested

## Capability model (illustrative)
- `figma.components.read`
- `figma.files.read` (for node references)

## FCP2 requirements
- Single-zone binding.
- NetworkConstraints pinned.
- Treat component names/descriptions as tainted.

## Deliverables
- Operation schemas:
  - `figma.component.list`
  - `figma.component.get`
  - `figma.component_set.list`
  - `figma.component.instances`

## Test strategy (MUST)
- Mock-only tests for:
  - listing pagination
  - large-file behavior (bounded)
  - error mapping (missing component, permission)

## Acceptance criteria
- Component discovery is reliable and does not require full-file downloads by default.
